```python
class Solution:
    def kthFactor(self, n: int, k: int) -> int:
        factors = []
        for i in range(1, int(n**0.5) + 1):
            if n % i == 0:
                factors.append(i)
                if i != n // i:
                    factors.append(n // i)
        factors.sort()
        return factors[k-1] if k <= len(factors) else -1
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(n))
space complexity: O(sqrt(n))
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates up to the square root of n to find all factors, then sorts and returns the kth factor, achieving a time complexity of O(sqrt(n)) and space complexity of O(sqrt(n)).
